summary: Verified search_memory Tool Loop Guard implementation in nxt8_graph.py - all 12 tests passed. The guard prevents search_memory from being called more than once per request.
critical_code_review_comments: AgentState TypedDict correctly includes tool_counts: Dict[str, int] (line 31)
tools_node increments counts[name] for each tool call BEFORE guard check (line 168)
Guard at lines 187-189 returns skipped result when name=='search_memory' and counts[name]>1
Return payload includes tool_counts in both early return (line 163) and full return (line 216)
Other tools (e.g., award_skill_points) are not affected by the guard - only search_memory is limited
context_for_next_testing_agent: search_memory loop guard is implemented and verified. The tools_node in nxt8_graph.py now tracks tool call counts per request and blocks search_memory after the first call, returning a skipped result with reason 'search_memory limit reached'. Test file test_search_memory_loop_guard.py covers all edge cases including: count initialization, count increment, guard blocking on 2nd call, first call allowed, other tools unaffected, return payload structure, and MAX_ITERATIONS unchanged.
rca_of_the_issue: Feature implementation verified: The runaway loop bug where search_memory could be called repeatedly (wasting tokens on empty memory) is now mitigated by the tool_counts guard. Implementation correctly: (1) tracks counts in AgentState, (2) increments before execution, (3) blocks execution when count > 1 for search_memory only, (4) returns informative skipped result.
